PLAINTIFFS' PAGA NOTICE LISTS THE SPECIFIC PROVISIONS OF THE LABOR CODE THAT DEFENDANTS ALLEGEDLY VIOLATED AND INCLUDES FACTS AND THEORIES TO SUPPORT THE ALLEGED VIOLATIONS, THEREBY SATISFYING THE REQUIREMENTS OF LABOR CODE SECTION 2699.3(A)(1)(A). LIBERALLY CONSTRUING THE COMPLAINT PER CCP SECTION 452, PLAINTIFFS ALLEGED SUFFICIENT ULTIMATE FACTS TO SUPPORT ALL OF THEIR CLAIMS. COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFFS IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. =302/HK

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-25-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT MAX'S SF OPERA CAFE, INC., MAX'S OPERA CAFE, INC., MAX'S OPERA INC., 601 MAX'S INC., MAX'S OPERA CAFE, DENNIS BERKOWIYZ, MAHMOOD AZAD, ALI AZAD, MEHRDAD HEJAZIAN's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Defendants' demurrer to all six causes of action in the first amended complaint is overruled as to all six causes of action. Plaintiffs' PAGA notice lists the specific provisions of the Labor Code that defendants allegedly violated and includes facts and theories to support the alleged violations, thereby satisfying the requirements of Labor Code section 2699.3(a)(1)(A). Liberally construing the complaint per CCP section 452, plaintiffs alleged sufficient ultimate facts to support all of their claims.

Counsel for plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600, Maria.Torreano@gmail.com, Not Reported. =(302/HK)
